```json
{
  "module": "database-api",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 35,
    "domain_depth": 55,
    "data_richness": 45,
    "interaction_quality": 40,
    "ai_integration": 30,
    "mobile_first": 25,
    "performance": 50,
    "typescript_quality": 58,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 45,
    "security": 52,
    "differentiation": 35,
    "enterprise_readiness": 48
  },
  "overall_score": 44,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "database-api-C3-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Activity feed enrichActivityItem and batchFetchEntityLabels silently swallow fetch errors",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/activity.ts",
      "description": "batchFetchEntityLabels pushes bare .then() promises into fetches array without .catch(). If any supabase query inside those .then() blocks throws, the error propagates as an unhandled rejection. Also, enrichActivityItem is referenced but not defined in the visible code, potentially causing runtime crashes when activity feed loads.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/activity.ts, in the batchFetchEntityLabels function: BEFORE: The fetches array contains promises created with `.then()` but no `.catch()`, meaning a failed RFI or submittal lookup causes an unhandled promise rejection that crashes the activity feed. AFTER: Wrap each `.then()` block with a trailing `.catch(() => { /* silently skip failed label lookups */ })` so that a single failed entity label fetch does not prevent the entire activity feed from loading. Specifically, after each `fetches.push(supabase.from('rfis')...then(...))` and `fetches.push(supabase.from('submittals')...then(...))`, append `.catch(() => {})`. Also add a try/catch around `await Promise.all(fetches)` that returns the partial labelMap on failure instead of throwing. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C3-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "Field endpoint parseJsonArray and type guards lack validation for daily log sub-entries",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/field.ts",
      "description": "The isVisitor type guard function body is cut off in the visible code, likely causing a TypeScript compilation error or incomplete runtime validation. Additionally, DailyLogPayload type is imported but the create/update functions for daily logs are not visible, suggesting mutations may be missing or incomplete for the daily log workflow which is a P0 field-first feature.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/field.ts, in the isVisitor function: BEFORE: The isVisitor type guard function is truncated and likely does not have a complete return statement, which would cause a TypeScript error or always return undefined (falsy). AFTER: Complete the isVisitor function body to validate all required Visitor fields: `return (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof (v as Visitor).name === 'string' && typeof (v as Visitor).company === 'string' && typeof (v as Visitor).purpose === 'string' && typeof (v as Visitor).time_in === 'string' && typeof (v as Visitor).time_out === 'string')`. Also add a missing isIncidentDetail type guard following the same pattern: `export function isIncidentDetail(v: unknown): v is IncidentDetail { return (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof (v as IncidentDetail).description === 'string' && typeof (v as IncidentDetail).type === 'string' && typeof (v as IncidentDetail).corrective_action === 'string') }`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C3-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "projectScope middleware caches access check for 5 seconds allowing stale permission",
      "file": "src/api/middleware/projectScope.ts",
      "description": "assertProjectAccess uses dedupTtl with a 5000ms TTL on project member lookups. The comment acknowledges this risk for write operations but does not enforce bypass. After role revocation, a user retains access for up to 5 seconds. For budget and pay app mutations this is a security gap. The function also catches all non-ApiError exceptions and converts them to 503, masking potential auth failures.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/middleware/projectScope.ts, in the assertProjectAccess function: BEFORE: The project member lookup is cached with dedupTtl(key, 5000, ...) which means after role revocation a user can still pass access checks for up to 5 seconds. The generic catch block converts unknown errors to 503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, masking potential permission errors. AFTER: Reduce the TTL from 5000 to 1000 (1 second) to minimize the stale access window while still deduplicating concurrent calls. In the catch block, before throwing the generic 503, add a check: `if (err instanceof Error && err.message.includes('permission')) throw new ApiError('Access denied', 403, 'FORBIDDEN')`. Add an exported function `assertProjectAccessNoCache(projectId: string)` that calls the same logic but bypasses dedupTtl entirely, for use by sensitive write operations. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C3-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "performance",
      "title": "getProject makes sequential await calls instead of parallel for schedule metrics",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/projects.ts",
      "description": "getProject uses Promise.all for project and schedule metrics (good), but getProjectMetrics in metrics.ts fetches a single row then computes health scores synchronously. The fetchScheduleMetrics function inside projects.ts makes a full table scan of schedule_phases without a limit, and computes critical path variance by sorting all critical items in memory. For projects with 500+ phases this is slow.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/projects.ts, in the fetchScheduleMetrics function: BEFORE: The query selects ALL schedule_phases for a project without filtering or limiting, then filters critical path items in JavaScript and sorts them in memory. For large projects with 500+ phases, this returns excessive data over the wire. AFTER: Add `.eq('is_critical_path', true)` to a separate query for critical path variance calculation, and use `.order('baseline_end', { ascending: false }).limit(1)` to get only the last critical path item directly from the database instead of fetching all and sorting in JS. For the completion percentage calculation, keep the full query but add `.select('baseline_start, baseline_end, percent_complete')` (remove is_critical_path from the select since it's not needed for that calculation) to reduce payload size. This changes the function to use two targeted queries instead of one broad query. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C3-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "Edit locks have no heartbeat mechanism causing stale locks after browser crash",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/editLocks.ts",
      "description": "The edit lock system uses a 5-minute expiry but has no heartbeat/renewal mechanism. If a user actively edits for more than 5 minutes, their lock expires and another user can acquire it, causing silent data overwrites. Conversely, if a browser crashes, the lock persists for the full 5 minutes blocking other users unnecessarily. There is no releaseLock function visible.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/editLocks.ts, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The LOCK_DURATION_MS is 5 minutes with no renewal or explicit release function. A user editing for >5 minutes loses their lock silently. A crashed browser leaves a stale lock for 5 minutes. AFTER: 1) Add an exported `renewEditLock` function that takes (entityType, entityId, userId) and updates expires_at to now + LOCK_DURATION_MS only if the current lock is held by userId. Return boolean success. 2) Add an exported `releaseEditLock` function that takes (entityType, entityId, userId) and deletes the lock row only if locked_by_user_id matches userId. 3) Reduce LOCK_DURATION_MS to 2 * 60 * 1000 (2 minutes) since the frontend should call renewEditLock every 60 seconds via setInterval. Add a comment documenting that the frontend must call renewEditLock on a 60-second interval. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's API layer uses a custom REST API with comprehensive rate limiting and versioning. SiteSync's direct Supabase client approach is faster to build but lacks request deduplication at scale, API versioning, and circuit breaker patterns. The edit lock system is a good differentiator but needs heartbeat support to match real collaborative editing tools like Figma or Google Docs. The batch entity label fetching in the activity feed is a smart optimization that Procore lacks (they require N+1 calls). Fieldwire has superior offline queue management; SiteSync's field endpoint needs explicit offline mutation queueing.",
  "summary": "The Database & API layer has solid architectural foundations — typed Supabase queries, project scoping middleware, RLS enforcement, and real-time subscriptions. However, five critical issues undermine reliability: the activity feed silently swallows errors from batch label fetches causing potential unhandled rejections; the field endpoint has truncated type guards that may break daily log parsing; the project access middleware caches permissions for too long creating a security window after role revocation; the project metrics query does a full table scan of schedule phases instead of targeted queries; and the edit lock system lacks heartbeat renewal and explicit release, causing both premature lock loss and stale lock blocking. Fixing these five issues will improve error resilience, security posture, and performance for the most critical data paths in the application."
}
```
